title: Treasury Internal Transfer Netting
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: treasury internal transfer netting.
assumptions: Internal ledger provides atomic holds and releases for debit/credit operations used in netting.
Settlement rails expose deterministic execution windows and deterministic finality semantics when invoked.
Approval tokens and clearing-agent identities are authoritative and uniquely represent consent.
Routing rules are precomputed and consistent with account-to-rail mappings.
risks: Liquidity shortfall when aggregated debits exceed available funding accounts leading to failed settlement.
Race conditions between routing/approval steps and final release causing double-release or missed holds.
Misrouting of netted settlement to inappropriate rail causing delayed or non-final settlement.
Incomplete or inconsistent account bindings leading to reconciliation failures and manual intervention.
Compliance or authorization lapses if approval tokens or clearing-agent validations are bypassed.
